conversation_history.question: Can you describe a project you led from start to finish, where you took ownership of the outcome? What was your role, what were some key challenges you faced, and how did you measure its impact?
conversation_history.answer: One project I led from start to finish was my movie recommendation system. I took full ownership of the project from planning and data set selection to model building, API integration and front end development. My role was not only as a developer but also as the project planner. I decided the features, selected the data set, designed the system flow and implemented both the back end and front end. The goal was to build a recommendation system that could suggest similar movies based on user preferences and also displayed detailed information like posters, ratings and trailers. One of the key challenges I faced was handling large and messy data sets. The TMDB data set contained missing values, inconsistent formats and multiple CSV files like movies metadata, ratings and credits. When I tried merging them initially, I encountered errors and incorrect recommendations. I solved this by performing detailed data cleaning using pandas, removing now values, correcting data types and carefully merging data sets based on common IDs. Another major challenge was improving recommendation accuracy. My first model used simple content based filtering but the results were not very personalized. So I enhanced the system by combining multiple features like genres, cast, keywords and overview text into a single feature vector and applied TFIDF with cosine similarity. This improved the relevance significantly. I also integrated the TMDB API to fetch real-time posters and trailers and I handled API errors by adding proper validations and fallback options. Through this project, I learned leadership, problem solving and end-to-end project ownership. It strengthened my technical skills and taught me how to build a complete user-focused system rather than just a model.
